distinctive_features: Regal attire indicative of a king
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Larger than average human, imposing stature
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Muscular build with a commanding presence

domain_specific_visual.WEAPONRY: Carries divine weapons like Brahmastra and Agneyastra
domain_specific_visual.CROWN: Wears a crown symbolizing his kingship

domain_specific_functional.MYTHOLOGICAL_ROLE: Symbolizes the struggle between good and evil
domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_IMPACT: Associated with the festival of Naraka Chaturdashi

relationships.description: Narakasura is the son of Bhudevi, the earth goddess.
Fathered by the Varaha incarnation of Vishnu.
Father of Bhagadatta, who fought in the Mahabharata.

contextual_attributes.historical_period: Referenced in texts from 1st century onwards
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Progenitor of dynasties in Kamarupa; associated with the Kamakhya temple
